verdicts.claim: public inverse-square routing is the realized allocation rule
price is a sufficient routing/welfare score
verdicts.reason: delivered quality explains 6.2% of residual within-model price variation; cheap-bad p=0.087

verdicts.claim: Brown-MacKay is the preferred competitive pricing null
verdicts.reason: requires temporal holdout gain plus cadence premium and reaction predictions
requires valid commitments, outcomes, costs, and randomized assignments

verdicts.claim: selfish optimization attains approximate global welfare
verdicts.reason: C1-C10 conjunction and causal policy comparison are required

claim_boundary: Failure of observed decentralization conditions means the welfare conclusion is not licensed; it is not a structural estimate of welfare loss. Brown-MacKay can be a preferred competitive null without being causally confirmed.
